disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Continuous convulsive activity for more than 5 minutes with no recovery of consciousness between movements
trigger_question_examples: How long has the shaking been going for?
Has he stopped or woken up at all since it started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It has been going for more than ten minutes straight and he hasn't come round or stopped even once.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Blue discolouration around the lips and noisy/irregular breathing during the episode (airway and oxygenation compromise)
trigger_question_examples: Does his breathing sound normal, or noisy and blocked?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His lips have gone blue and his breathing is really noisy and gurgly, like it's blocked.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known epilepsy; regular anti-seizure medication ran out three days ago and none taken since
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
Is he on any regular medications, and has he been taking them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has epilepsy. He takes a daily seizure tablet but he ran out three days ago and hasn't had any.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: This episode is markedly different from and longer than his usual self-limiting seizures
trigger_question_examples: How is this one different from his usual seizures?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His fits normally stop by themselves in a minute or two and he wakes up. This one just won't stop and he won't wake up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He is completely unresponsive and cannot protect his own airway; has been incontinent of urine
trigger_question_examples: Is he responding to you at all?
Can he talk or follow you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing at all — he can't hear me, he's totally out, and he's wet himself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No rescue seizure medication or seizure action plan volunteered; carer does not know what to do
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any emergency rescue medicine for seizures, like a buccal or nasal midazolam?
Is there a seizure action plan from his neurologist?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't think so — I've never given him anything like that. We just wait for them to stop normally.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not sure, I can't think straight right now — what should I be doing?
